comments.author: @brossmart
comments.text:
What pisses me off about westerners, they think they can come in and score with baltic women to have sex with them. I may be a westerner, but I have to say that youtube speaking about shortages of men and beautifull women outnumbering men have caught my eye, misleading me to think I'd have better luck over there......not only was I wrong about my luck and it not only hasn't changed with women my type cause I was still under the same curse plaging me at home, and I'm still cursed anywhere I go !!! And going to Latvia changed my perspective of finding anyone compatible but I also lost what most westerner's believe that women elsewhere are easier to approach and have sex with. That wasn't my plan going there, but one single neuron of mine though my luck could change elsewhere....oh god was I wrong into thinking I'd have better luck over the Atlantic, my fucking dark cloud followed me allllllllllllll the way there !!!! I was wrong thonking that flying over the clouds I could get rid of it.......nope it stuck with me and is still looming over me !!!!!

As I was digging deeper, many other interests popped up: culture, landscape, way of life, good food, plenty of services and very nice people but also Latvia is a clean place to visit. I may have never gotten laid but I don't give a fuck, I had a better experience because of it.  Coming out of the airport, I went to buy bus ticket for the week, the stall lady (I reserved the same touch as the others later down), greated her in Latvian asking her if she spoke English and yes of course she did. I told her I'd try to buy bus ticket in Latvian doing my best, she looked surprised but even with slight possible mispronunciations and grammar mistakes(nothing worthy to call the Latvian grammar police over !!!), she smiled at my efforts and proceeded for the transaction obviously touched by my efforts. I have to say it was one of my first contacts.
Upon taking the bus coming out of the airport and a few kilometers in, I asked two beautiful ladies: Sorry ladies, do you speak English (in Latvian of course, and yes they did speak English) asking them which bus station to get to the old town so I could do my check in at my hotel. The respect I gave them made them friendly and they accepted to help me and looked at their phone and gave me one. Few km later one tapped on my shoulder, ''I was wrong, it's this one you should take. Said thank you in Latvian and both smiled. They were absolutely aware I was a tourist BUT obviously enjoyed the fact I did try to speak their language....probaly why they felt compelled to correct their initial suggestion. Still another exemple of how respect towards people generally gets you further.

I'm French canadian, from Quebec, I even got served twice in my own native language: French !!!!!! Once stepping into the old town, I met a street vender and she was my first contact on the street there. I started greeting her in Latvian(as I have studied a bit of the language's basics before going there !!) asking her if she speaks English and yes she did. I also asked her respectfully to film what she was selling to send to my 2 daughters and she agreed. As I spoke French while filming  the ember jewelry in her stall, she turned around while holding something she was hanging up on a wall and spoke back to me in French: ''Vous parlez français ??'' !!!!!!!  I got served in my own language, not a crappy French with some accent you can't understand no, a good accentless French like a international French with good punctuations and everything !! I was flabbergasted and almost lost my own French, I was at a lost for words !!!!! She was actually fluent in Latvian, English, French, Latvian and Russian !!       I bought many pieces from her and even got a discount out of it !!!
The next one was a few days later I was looking for some sealable containers to put spices in for the traval back home(travel I was dreading already actualy !!!!!) and found some behind the pavillion outside in the ''bazar'''. I got in there, again  asking in Latvian if they speak English, the lady said yes (she was russian judging by her accent) and proceeded to ask for the containers( containers of which she, as a matter of fact, had in store) as she was gathering them, I looked around and spoke french, I didnd't belive I spoke loud enough for her to hear me but she actually did.........she turned around and immediately stopped speaking English ad started to speak to me in French, although bent a bit byt her russian accent, she had actually a very good understanding and hability to speak it as well !!!! I almost fainted of surprise !!!!!!!! I greated her three times, once in Latvian as I arrived, a second in French and a third in russian as I left. The surprise was now hers hearing me to speak russian a bit, even if it was ''just a thank you''. Told them both I was a French canadian so they knew I ws a tourist, even if it was kinda obvious.....they know tourists come fro all over, but werterners speaking a estern language other than English may have some effect.

All iin all  the food experience was out of this wold, the quality was unbelievable. My breakfast at the hotel filled me so much I barely could eat anything before dinner no matter what I ate in the morning. And I walked all over town all day !!!!!! One more thing, there is almost no obesity in Latvia !!!!!! People there look healthy, full of energy and enjoying good food.
Cleanliness ?? Way cleaner than here in the North Americas I'll tell you that !!!!!! Thrash bins are hard to find and when you do find one it's never full...yet no thrash laying around, no gums stuck on sidewalks, botches of cigarette even are very hard to find(might have found something like a few walking all over the old town, it was impressive to see compared to the North Americas(Canada/USA).  And the pavilions are worthy, you may find fresh local produce there,  food stalls, clothes, house articles and souvenir shops. Also worthy, the stalls may look stained, especially the ceramic behind the counters but it's a trap. It is clean and well maintained but years of washing have taken it's toll on it. The windows are light reflecting clean, my running shoes were squeeking on the floor at the end of the day !! According to some locals I spole to as I was impressed by the sanitation here, their ''sanitation agency or departement(not to be confused with the garbage collecting sanitation here, but the one who visits food stores/sellers, restaurants/hotels) is extremely severe about sanitation, it is no joke !! The fines and other disciplinary actions are not worthy of taking the risks, or any for that matter !!! The fish pavilion had a fresh sturgeon with its gills still moving, how any fresher could this be ??? Outside I bought a strawberry, though I wasn't hungry I had to try one !! Not only was it the sweetest and juiciest strawberry I ever ate, but I couldn't even compare to those back home, their strawberries are a lot less bitter, thankfully I had a napkin on me cause I could have stained my clothes so juicy dripping they were !! And their watermelon are huge compared to ours...huge, bright red and dripping also with seed.....watermelons cannot be any better !!! Ours are pinker, softer, fractured, full of hormones and whatnot chemicals and some in the states are even worse, they're like jelly of which you can bend the watermelon without breaking it !!! 🤮🤮
Public toilets are not free, you must pay for them and at less than a euro, you can afford clean seats/stalls !!! Even the flowerbeds are free of weeds,people are maintaining their topiary insanely clean and well maintained !!!! Beside the broken glass sometime you find around bars, there's not much to be offended for and mistakes happens when people drink !!!!

comments.author: @brossmart
comments.text:
True for black balsam !!!!! Think of it as a fruity porto kinda liquor. Come in original at 45% alcohol !! The black currant, cherry and my favorite: tropical at 30% !!!!!! 
Also worthy to mention is their garlicky rye bread dipped in a garlic dip. It would ruin your breath for a few days but it's worth it !!!!! 😋😋

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: It is wonderful to have you tuning in from Sri Lanka, thank you for the support.

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Thank you so much for watching from there. I’m really happy to have you joining this journey to Latvia with us. Have you ever visited Europe or the Baltic countries before?

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: Latvia is a fascinating mix of old European traditions, beautiful landscapes, and modern life. I enjoyed discovering the culture and everyday stories behind a country many people overlook.

comments.author: @GlobalRealLifeDocumentary
comments.text: Latvia feels like one of Europe's best-kept secrets—fairy-tale forests, beautiful old towns, peaceful beaches, and a rich culture that deserves far more attention. 🇱🇻 After watching this documentary, do you think hidden destinations like Latvia are better than Europe's famous tourist hotspots? Why or why not?

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: It’s fascinating how Latvians deeply value their personal space in daily life, yet every 5 years, tens of thousands of them gather to sing in perfect harmony at the Song and Dance Festival. That transition from quiet introverts to a massive, powerful choir is pure magic. Seeing Latvia thrive in 2026 while keeping these ancient roots alive is beautiful.
